questions.objective_ref: Explain what a Python exception is and how it differs from a syntax error — it is a runtime error that stops execution unless caught
Write a try block that wraps code which might fail, and an except block that handles the failure without crashing the program
Catch a specific exception rather than using bare except, which silences every possible error and makes debugging nearly impossible
Use the else clause to write code that runs only when the try block succeeded with no exception
Wrap file open() calls in a try/except FileNotFoundError block to gracefully handle missing or unreadable files
